questions.text: What is the primary trade-off when enabling Gradient Checkpointing during fine-tuning?
options: It reduces peak memory usage but increases total compute time by approximately 30%.
It restricts the model to 2 heads.
It requires retraining the base tokenizer.
questions.correct_answer: It reduces peak memory usage but increases total compute time by approximately 30%.
questions.explanation: Because missing activations must be re-computed during the backward pass, the compute overhead increases by roughly 30%.
